---
title: "5c: Functional Enrichments"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Summary

GO term enrichment analysis identifies biological functions that are significantly
over-represented in each phylon's gene set (hypergeometric test, p < 0.05).

```{python}
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5c_phylon_go_enrichment.csv")
print(f"Significant enrichments: {len(df)}")
print(f"Phylons with enrichments: {df['phylon'].nunique()}")
print(f"Unique GO terms: {df['function'].nunique()}")
```

## Top Enrichments

```{python}
df[['phylon', 'function', 'name', 'p_value', 'overlap', 'genes in phylon', 'genes w function']].head(20)
```

## Enrichment Heatmap

Clustered heatmap of enrichment p-values (phylons x GO terms). Darker colors
indicate stronger enrichment (lower p-values).

![](../output/figures/5c_enrichment_heatmap.png){width=90%}

## Phylon Wordclouds

Word clouds of gene product descriptions for each phylon, weighted by
phylon contribution. Common uninformative terms (hypothetical, uncharacterized,
etc.) are filtered out.

```{python}
import os
from pathlib import Path
wc_files = sorted(Path("../output/figures/").glob("5c_wordcloud_phylon*.png"))
for wc in wc_files:
    phylon = wc.stem.replace("5c_wordcloud_", "")
    print(f"### {phylon}")
```

```{python}
#| output: asis
for wc in wc_files:
    phylon = wc.stem.replace("5c_wordcloud_", "")
    print(f"### {phylon}\n")
    print(f"![](../{wc}){{width=60%}}\n")
```
